Title: Specific gut microbiome signatures and the associated pro-inflammatory functions are linked to pediatric allergy and acquisition of immune tolerance

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA706116); HUMAnN3 functional profiling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: O-antigen/teichoic acid export membrane protein family
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pro-inflammatory microbial molecule linked to cytokine induction and inflammation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Gut microbial cell envelope / gut lumen
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic functional profiling and differential abundance analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher abundance in allergic children indicates increased microbial potential for LPS-related biosynthesis/export
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pediatric food or respiratory allergy status
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UniRef_A0A395J976, annotated as O-antigen/teichoic acid export membrane protein, was enriched in allergic children. 2. This supports increased metagenomic potential for LPS-associated functions. 3. The enrichment contributes to the pro-inflammatory signature of allergic microbiomes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Community-level metagenomic feature; not assigned to a specific strain in this paper.

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA706116); HUMAnN3 functional profiling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pro-inflammatory microbial molecule linked to inflammation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Gut microbial cell envelope / gut lumen
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic functional profiling and differential abundance analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher abundance in allergic children indicates increased microbial capacity for LPS biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pediatric food or respiratory allergy status
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UPI000F05499B, annotated as an LPS biosynthesis protein, was enriched in allergic children. 2. This supports an increased capacity for pro-inflammatory LPS production. 3. The feature helps define the allergy-associated functional microbiome profile.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Community-level feature from HUMAnN3 rather than a single isolated gene study.

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: Ruminococcus gnavus pro-inflammatory polysaccharide biosynthetic genes (23-gene set)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA706116); assembly and BlastX mapping to 23 reported genes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pro-inflammatory molecule associated with allergic/inflammatory responses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher prevalence in allergy-associated R. gnavus strains
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted metagenomic mapping of 23 biosynthetic genes; strain-level pangenome analysis; machine-learning classification
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allergic children harbored more hits to R. gnavus genes involved in pro-inflammatory polysaccharide production
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pediatric food or respiratory allergy status; single versus multiple allergen sensitization
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with inflammation; also linked with reduced fiber-degrading capacity in allergy-associated strains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Allergy-associated R. gnavus strains carried more genes from a 23-gene cluster involved in biosynthesis of a pro-inflammatory polysaccharide. 2. This gene set discriminated healthy from allergic children with good classification performance. 3. The findings support strain-dependent inflammatory functions of R. gnavus in pediatric allergy.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats the 23 genes as a biosynthetic signature rather than naming each gene individually.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA706116); functional profiling by HUMAnN3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes urea to ammonia and carbon dioxide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with pro-inflammatory gut environment and dysbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Shotgun metagenomic functional profiling and differential abundance analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher abundance of urease genes in allergic children indicates increased microbial potential for ammonia production
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pediatric food or respiratory allergy status
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with pro-inflammatory potential and gut dysbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbiome-targeting biomarker or intervention target to reduce pro-inflammatory metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Urease genes were enriched in the gut metagenome of allergic children versus healthy controls. 2. This indicates increased microbial potential for urea degradation and ammonia production. 3. The enrichment contributes to the pro-inflammatory functional signature associated with pediatric allergy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper analyzes community-level microbial genes rather than a single defined locus.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Ruminococcus gnavus strains enriched in healthy children
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA706116); PanPhlAn strain pangenome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes acetyl groups from xylan, facilitating fiber degradation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Support epithelial integrity and immune tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present in healthy-associated R. gnavus strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pan-genome presence/absence analysis of R. gnavus genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Healthy-associated R. gnavus strains were enriched for acetylxylan esterase genes, consistent with greater fiber-degrading capacity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss in allergy-associated strains may reduce fiber use and SCFA generation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for selecting beneficial fiber-degrading microbiome strains
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Acetylxylan esterase genes were among R. gnavus pangenes enriched in healthy children. 2. Their presence is consistent with improved complex polysaccharide degradation. 3. Loss of these genes in allergy-associated strains suggests reduced contribution to SCFA-supporting metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inferred as a strain-associated functional gene, not experimentally assayed individually.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Ruminococcus gnavus strains enriched in healthy children
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA706116); PanPhlAn strain pangenome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes terminal alpha-L-fucosyl residues from glycans
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Support epithelial integrity and immune tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present in healthy-associated R. gnavus strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pan-genome presence/absence analysis of R. gnavus genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Healthy-associated R. gnavus strains carried more alpha-L-fucosidase genes, indicating better glycan degradation potential
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss in allergy-associated strains may reduce carbohydrate utilization breadth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for beneficial gut strains with enhanced glycan utilization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Alpha-L-fucosidase genes were enriched in healthy-associated R. gnavus strains. 2. These genes support complex glycan degradation capacity. 3. Their depletion in allergy-associated strains is consistent with reduced fiber/glycan catabolism in allergic microbiomes.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Ruminococcus gnavus strains enriched in healthy children
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA706116); PanPhlAn strain pangenome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Support epithelial integrity and immune tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present in healthy-associated R. gnavus strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pan-genome presence/absence analysis of R. gnavus genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Healthy-associated R. gnavus strains were enriched in beta-xylosidase genes, consistent with enhanced fiber degradation capacity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Depletion in allergy-associated strains may contribute to lower SCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biomarker for selecting fiber-degrading microbiome configurations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Beta-xylosidase genes were detected among healthy-associated R. gnavus pangenes. 2. They indicate stronger xylan degradation potential. 3. Their depletion in allergy-associated strains supports reduced fiber catabolism in allergic children.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not individually functionally validated in vivo.
